definition_population.content: 'Immune system disorder' is a broad disease-ontology umbrella label, not a single defined clinical entity; no unified epidemiology or natural history applies.

epidemiology.content: Not established in this pass; the label is non-specific and returns an extremely large heterogeneous ClinicalTrials.gov result set rather than mapping to one condition.

biology.content: PLA2G4A (cPLA2alpha) regulates TNF-induced inflammatory effectors in rheumatoid arthritis synoviocytes and has been linked to eosinophilic pediatric asthma phenotypes; PDIA6 is an ER protein-folding enzyme shown in a mouse forward genetic screen to be essential for lymphoid and myeloid development.

sources.quote: Cytosolic phospholipase A2 regulates TNF-induced production of joint destructive effectors in synoviocytes.
Association between PLA2G4A and P2RX7 genes and eosinophilic phenotype and environment with pediatric asthma.
syndromic disorder marked by growth retardation, diabetes, premature death, and severe lymphoid and myeloid hypoplasia together with diminished T cell-independent (TI) antibody responses

treatment.content: No FDA-approved disease-specific therapy identified in this pass; no PLA2G4A/PDIA6-targeted drug application found.

regulatory_trials.content: ClinicalTrials.gov search on 'immune system disorder' returned 48,030 total trial records, reflecting broad umbrella usage rather than a discrete indication.

gaps: Label is non-specific; no disease-level epidemiology found
No FDA-approved therapy identified for either target
No standout Phase 3 trial attributable to this generic label
